title: Thomas R. Eisenmann endorses Incorruptible
summary: Harvard's Thomas Eisenmann calls Incorruptible Eric Ries at his most ambitious, arguing corruption results from institutional flaws that can be engineered away.
blockquote: Incorruptible is Eric Ries at his most ambitious and original. He argues that corruption — extracting value from successful companies — results not from moral failure but from flaws in our institutions. Ries shows how organizations can be engineered to resist these flaws. This is a practical manifesto for building mission-driven companies that survive success without killing the golden goose.
attribution_title: Howard H. Stevenson Professor, Harvard Business School
